claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
circumstances: Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the children you're asking about.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm asking about. His name is Alex and he's 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

Now I need to ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility:

**Are you responsible for Alex? This means do you live with him and are responsible for his care and upbringing?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me. He lives with his other parent.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to understand the situation better.

**Do you contribute towards Alex's upbringing in any way? For example, do you pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit or the equivalent rate towards his upkeep?**

(Child Benefit is currently £25.60 per week for the eldest/only child, or £16.95 per week for additional children.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially towards his upkeep.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me ask one more question to complete the assessment:

**Is there any other reason or circumstance that would mean you're responsible for Alex, such as a formal arrangement or agreement that gives you parental responsibility even though he doesn't live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, there's no formal arrangement like that. His other parent has him living there and looks after him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me, I need to make my assessment based on the UK Child Benefit eligibility rules.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Result

Unfortunately, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

While Alex is 8 years old (which meets the age requirement of being under 16), to claim Child Benefit you must be **responsible for the child**. This generally means:
- You live with the child, OR
- You contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount towards the child's upkeep

Since Alex lives with his other parent, you don't contribute financially to his upkeep, and there's no formal arrangement giving you responsibility for him, you don't meet the eligibility criteria.

**The other parent who Alex lives with and who is responsible for his care would be the person eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if Alex comes to live with you, or you start contributing financially towards his upkeep), you may become eligible at that point.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. However, to be eligible for Child Benefit, you must be responsible for the child. This generally means you must live with the child and be responsible for their care and upbringing. Alex does not live with you - he lives with his other parent. You are not contributing financially towards Alex's upkeep (at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit rate). There is no formal arrangement giving you responsibility for Alex. Since you do not live with Alex, are not responsible for his care, and do not contribute financially to his upkeep, you do not meet the responsibility requirement for Child Benefit. The other parent who Alex lives with and who looks after him would be the person eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 8), who lives with the other parent. The claimant does not live with Alex, does not contribute financially to his upkeep, and has no formal arrangement for responsibility. Therefore, the claimant does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit as they are not responsible for Alex.
